skill1.kitLine: Activates every time EXP stacks 10. Affects self.
skill1.where: note (MODEL paragraph): '10 EXP = +1 Hero Level, cap 11 (~30 hits/level)'; encoded as hitCount 30 on the reload + heal blocks
skill1.note: The 10-EXP-per-level trigger is collapsed into hitCount 30 (= 10 EXP × 3 hits/EXP). No standalone block; the cadence is baked into the two hitCount-30 blocks.

skill1.kitLine: Hero Level Up: Reaches a maximum of Level 11.
skill1.where: note (MODEL paragraph): 'cap 11'; encoded in the level-11 steady-state aura values (1.16×11, 0.91×11) and burst DoT (20.87×11)
skill1.note: No explicit cap field. The cap is expressed by computing all Hero-Level-scaled values at level 11.

skill1.where: skill1 block 2: hitCount 30, self, instantReload fraction 0.1026
skill1.note: Preserved verbatim from partial-hand model. Note says 'level-up rewards KEEP TRIGGERING at max level (review-confirmed)'.

skill1.kitLine: Hero Level Up Reward: Recovers 2.44% of the skill user's final Max HP.
skill1.where: skill1 block 3: hitCount 30, self, heal event
skill1.note: NEW additive block (HARD RULE 2). Previously a SILENT DROP; now event-only heal so recovery-consumer teammates (e.g. Crown) see the proc. Zero self-damage impact.

skill1.kitLine: Activates when Hero levels up. Affects all Water Code allies.
skill1.where: note (MODEL paragraph): 'level-11 steady-state auras are passive full-value from t=0'; encoded as passive trigger on skill1 block 1
skill1.note: The per-level-up ramp is collapsed to a passive at full level-11 value. Measured-accurate per residual (⚑3).

skill1.kitLine: Elemental Advantage Attack Damage ▲ 1.16% * Hero Level continuously.
skill1.where: skill1 block 1: passive, alliesOfElement Water, elemAdvantageDamagePct 12.76 (= 1.16 × 11)

skill1.kitLine: ATK ▲ 0.91% of the skill user's ATK * Hero Level continuously.
skill1.where: skill1 block 1: passive, alliesOfElement Water, casterAtkPct 10.01 (= 0.91 × 11)

skill2.kitLine: Activates after landing 6 normal attack(s) without hitting the core. Affects self.
skill2.where: skill2 block 1 (hitCount 3) jointly represents both EXP branches; see note ⚑2
skill2.note: Non-core branch (6 hits) never fires at scope-lock 100% core exposure. Represented via the core-branch hitCount-3 block under that assumption. Blend for c<1 is documented ⚑2 (unmeasured estimate).

skill2.kitLine: EXP: ATK ▲ 1.81%, stacks up to 100 time(s) continuously. (non-core branch)
skill2.where: skill2 block 1: hitCount 3, self, atkPct 1.81, maxStacks 100
skill2.note: Same block as core branch. Value 1.81% and cap 100 are identical across both branches.

skill2.kitLine: Activates when hitting the Core for 3 time(s). Affects self.
skill2.note: AUTHORED fix: was previously materialized at hitCount 6 (non-core branch). Corrected to 3 (core branch) for scope-lock consistency.

skill2.kitLine: EXP: ATK ▲ 1.81%, stacks up to 100 time(s) continuously. (core branch)
skill2.note: Engine ramps the stack natively; cap at ~32s wall, no haircut needed per note.

skill2.kitLine: Activates when Hero Level is 2 or above. Affects self.
skill2.where: note (MODEL paragraph): 'Hero Level 2 or above → passive (level 2 at ~3s in; first-3s over-credit negligible)'
skill2.note: Trigger condition collapsed to passive. Caveat 3 documents the approximation.
Passive from t=0 instead of gated on Hero Level 2 (~3s). Over-credit negligible per note.

burst.where: burst block 1: trigger burstCast, target alliesOfElement Water

burst.kitLine: Elemental Advantage Attack Damage ▲ 18.75% for 10 sec.
Affects 1 enemy unit(s) with the highest final Max HP.
burst.note: Scope-lock is partless single boss, so 'highest final Max HP' targeting is trivially the boss. No targeting logic needed.

burst.kitLine: Deals continuous damage equal to 20.87% of the final ATK * Hero Level every sec for 10 sec.
burst.where: burst block 2: dot atkPct 229.57 (= 20.87 × 11), durationSec 10, intervalSec 1
burst.note: Preserved verbatim. Level 11 guaranteed by first burst at 40s CD. Measured-accurate per residual.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1/skill2: normal-fire cadence (12/s, reload 81f) is datamined and measured ~26% hot — flagged for video measurement, deliberately NOT refit
documentedConfounds.why: This is the DOMINANT issue per kit-status findings. Board reads 1.070 HOT. The datamined rate_of_fire 720 (12/s) + 81f reload produces ~26% over-credit in normal-fire DPS. Owner ruling: do NOT refit by fudge; pin via focus video. Arithmetic suspect: 12/1.26 ≈ 9.5/s effective.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: EXP build rate modeled at 3 hits/stack (core branch at scope-lock 100% core exposure); the blend with the 6-hit non-core branch is an unmeasured estimate
documentedConfounds.why: At scope-lock (100% core exposure, partless boss), the core branch (3 hits) is the only one that fires. The blend only matters if core fraction < 1, which is out of scope. No gap expected at scope-lock.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Elemental Advantage Attack Damage 7.46% modeled as passive (Hero Level 2 is reached ~3s into the fight)
documentedConfounds.why: First-3s over-credit of 7.46% elemAdvantage on a 180s fight is negligible (~0.04% of total fight time). No meaningful gap.

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑1 (TOP, ESCALATED): pullsPerSec 12 + reloadFrames 81 cadence tuple needs video measurement — same root cause as caveat 1
documentedConfounds.why: Duplicate of caveat 1 from the note's ⚑ list. The measured residual already reads ~26% HOT with these values. This is the single largest known source of inaccuracy.

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑2: EXP build blend — if real core-hit fraction c<1, effective hits/stack ∈ [3,6]
documentedConfounds.why: At scope-lock c=1 (100% core), so effective = 3 hits/stack exactly. Only relevant out-of-scope.

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑3 (low): level-11 steady-state auras are passive full-value from t=0 (no ~25s ramp haircut) — measured-accurate per residual
documentedConfounds.why: Residual confirms burst DoT + Hero-Level auras are measured accurate. The ~25s ramp is a small fraction of 180s and the residual validates the approximation.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1 heal block is NEW additive (HARD RULE 2) — previously a SILENT DROP; driver may strip if strict-verbatim wins
documentedConfounds.why: The heal has zero self-damage impact. It exists only so recovery-consumer teammates see the proc. Stripping it would not change GWS's own damage numbers.

authorIntentSummary: The override models GWS as a Hero-Level-stacking Water Attacker with three preserved/measured-accurate components (skill1 Water-ally auras at level-11 steady state, skill1 reload-on-level cadence, burst buffs + DoT) and one authored component (skill2 EXP ATK stacks via the corrected core-branch hitCount 3, fixing a prior materialization bug that used the non-core hitCount 6). Every kit line is represented — no silent drops remain (the 2.44% Max HP heal was a former silent drop, now an event-only block). The single dominant known gap is the normal-fire cadence: datamined rate_of_fire 720 + 81f reload reads ~26% HOT on the board (ratio 1.070), and the owner has explicitly ruled against fudge-refitting — it awaits video measurement.
